statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function to validate the structure of an HTML image tag. The input string will contain the start of the tag, the image URL, and the end of the tag. The function should return `true` if the tag is valid and `false` otherwise. A valid tag should start with `<img`, followed by any number of spaces, and then have an `src` attribute with the image URL, and finally end with `/>`.
statement.notes: The input string will not contain any other attributes besides `src`. The function should only check the structure of the tag, not the content.

examples.explanation: The tag is valid because it starts with `<img`, has a `src` attribute, and ends with `/>`.
The tag is invalid because it does not end with `/>`.
The tag is invalid because it starts with `<image` instead of `<img`.

hints.text: Check the start of the tag to ensure it's `<img`.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Invalid HTML Image Tags
editorial.intuition: The function should check the start and end of the tag, and ensure that it has a `src` attribute.
editorial.bruteForce: The function can iterate over the input string to check the structure of the tag.
editorial.optimalSolution: The function can use regular expressions to check the structure of the tag.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return `true` for valid tags and `false` for invalid tags.
commonMistakes: Not checking the end of the tag
Not checking the start of the tag
